stem: A urban planning study measures the commute time (minutes) for a sample of 20 city workers. The sample mean is 282.9 min with a sample standard deviation of 40.4 min. Assuming the data are approximately normally distributed, estimate the true population mean and provide a 95% confidence interval.

ground_truth.answer: Population mean ≈ 282.9 min. 95% CI = [264.0, 301.8]. Point estimate: 282.9 min.

common_failure_modes: Using z-critical value instead of t-critical for small samples
Not reporting the confidence interval alongside the point estimate
